release_health.reason: No red blockers are open. Foundation release remains closed and the active work is additive middle-layer and trust-surface depth.

top_support_or_feedback_clusters.summary: Campaign Breadth and Promotion is the post-next20 additive product-pressure cluster: broaden campaign return, creator publication, public promotion, and trust-surface follow-through without reopening closed boundary or control-plane work.
Downloads, updates, support closure, and channel-aware trust copy now exist as first-party surfaces and must keep moving in lockstep instead of drifting back into separate promises.
The current longest pole remains Cloud & Publishing, so release, support, and publication decisions should assume that this lane still sets the pacing risk for the broader public product.

governor_decisions.reason: Keep the recommended wave on Campaign Breadth and Promotion now that the Next 20 Big Wins program is materially closed. The pulse shows 73% overall progress in 'Scale & stabilize', history depth has reached 2 snapshots, and the pacing risk is still concentrated in Cloud & Publishing rather than in reopened foundation blockers.

snapshot.next_checkpoint_question: What is the smallest cross-repo slice that makes campaign breadth, creator trust, and public promotion feel undeniably real to users?
